entries.value: Target consultant utilization rate is 75-85% of available hours billed to clients. Below 70% signals bench time or poor sales pipeline. Above 90% risks burnout and quality degradation.
Revenue per consultant benchmarks: junior $150-250K/yr, senior $300-500K/yr, partner $500K-1M+/yr. Below benchmark indicates pricing or utilization issues.
Target project gross margin is 40-60% for strategy work, 30-45% for implementation. Margin below 25% on any engagement warrants immediate review of scope creep and staffing mix.
Target client NPS is 50+. Score 70+ is world-class. Below 30 indicates systemic delivery quality issues. Survey at engagement midpoint and close for actionable feedback.
Target knowledge reuse rate is 30-50% of deliverables leveraging existing artifacts. Below 20% means the knowledge base is underutilized. Above 60% may indicate over-templating without customization.
Alert when engagement spend exceeds 80% of budget with less than 60% of deliverables complete. Critical when spend exceeds 100% of budget. Budget overruns erode margin and client trust.
All consultants must submit time entries within 24 hours. Compliance below 90% degrades billing accuracy and cash flow. Weekly reminders at 85%, escalation at 80%.
Optimal staffing mix for strategy engagements: 1 partner (10-15% time), 1 manager (40-60%), 2-3 analysts (80-100%). Inverted pyramids (too many seniors) destroy margin.
All client-facing deliverables require at least one peer review before delivery. Strategy reports need partner sign-off. Target review turnaround: 48 hours.

Engagement stages: Proposal (scoping and pricing) -> Active (work in progress) -> On Hold (paused, client-driven) -> Completed (all deliverables accepted) -> Cancelled (terminated early). Track days-in-stage for pipeline velocity.
